seoTitle: How to Send Money From Google Pay to PayPal (2026)
metaDescription: There's no direct transfer button, but here's how to bridge funds from Google Pay to PayPal using a shared bank account as a free transactional link.
intro: There isn't a one-click way to move money directly from a Google Pay balance into PayPal, but you can still get it done through a shared bank account. This guide shows how to withdraw from Google Pay and then pull those same funds into your PayPal wallet. It's useful for anyone consolidating money across multiple digital wallets.

steps.heading: Check your Google Pay balance
steps.body: Open the Google Pay app and confirm the amount you want to move sitting in your cash balance.

steps.heading: Transfer out to your bank
steps.body: Tap Transfer Out, choose your linked bank account, and pick between a standard or instant transfer speed.

steps.heading: Confirm the withdrawal
steps.body: Enter your transfer amount and confirm the transaction to route the funds out of your Google Wallet.

steps.heading: Log in to PayPal and add money
steps.body: Open PayPal, go to your balance module, and tap Add Money to pull funds from your bank account.

steps.heading: Select the same bank account and confirm
steps.body: Choose the same shared bank account you used with Google Pay to complete the inward transfer, then check your refreshed PayPal balance.

faq.question: Can I skip the bank account entirely?
faq.answer: Not currently — there's no direct wallet-to-wallet transfer between Google Pay and PayPal, so a shared bank account is the standard bridge.

faq.question: How long does the whole process take?
faq.answer: It depends on whether you choose standard or instant transfers on each end, but it can range from a few minutes to a couple of business days.

faq.question: Are there fees for moving money this way?
faq.answer: Standard bank transfers are typically free, while instant transfers on either platform may carry a small fee.

faq.question: Do I need the same bank account linked to both apps?
faq.answer: Yes, using the same account on both ends keeps the transfer traceable and avoids delays from mismatched account details.
